SEATTLE, June l0.-It is now be-
lieved in local railroad circles that
the recent recognition by high Canadian
Pacific officials OF the proposed Corbin
road from Spokane to the British Co
lumbia line, as a Spokane connection
Npf the Canadian Pacific indicates that
the latter line has practically ADAM
doned its plan of constructing branch
lines to Spokane and Seattle through
q company organized for that purpose.


The Canadian Pacific has, since the
formation of the Washington company,
effected traffic agreement with the
Northern Pacific extending over a long
period of years, by which it is able to
operate both passenger and freight
trains between Vancouver and this
city. This arrangement removes the
necessity for the construction of the
Seattle branch. The Spokane connec-
ton scheme has been replaced by the
proposed Corbin line, thus giving the
company facilities for caring for its
business without the necessity of build
ing either of the proposed branches.
